sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I understand! SPACE switches control between the two crosses. The white dot moved from the blue cross center to the yellow cross center at [27, 21]. The yellow cross is now broken into 4 separate 11-pixel arms (nodes 11, 17, 19, 20), and the blue cross is at the old yellow cross position.
Wait, let me re-examine. The blue cross (node 3, 53 pixels) has a boundary that includes the position [18, 48] area - that's where the blue cross was before. But the white dot is now at [27, 21] which is the yellow cross center.
So it seems like:
- SPACE switches the active cross
- The white dot moves to the other cross's center
- The previously active cross stays where it is
- The newly activated cross is now controllable
Now I control the yellow cross at [27, 21]. The yellow targets are at [3, 15], [9, 6], [9, 24], [17, 15].
But wait - the yellow cross arms are now separate pieces (4 nodes of 11 pixels each). Let me check if they move together when I press arrow keys.
Actually, looking more carefully at the yellow cross parts:
- Node 11: [[16, 21], [26, 21]] - vertical upper arm (11 pixels)
- Node 17: [[27, 10], [27, 20]] - horizontal left arm (11 pixels)
- Node 19: [[27, 22], [27, 32]] - horizontal right arm (11 pixels)
- Node 20: [[28, 21], [38, 21]] - vertical lower arm (11 pixels)
These form a cross centered at [27, 21] with arms extending 10 pixels from center (11 pixels each, not including center).
Also interesting - there are some charcoal pieces (nodes 8, 9) at [[15, 47], [17, 47]] and [[15, 49], [17, 49]] - these seem to be remnants of where the blue cross was or something related to the target at [35, 48].
Let me now try to collect yellow targets with the yellow cross. I need to figure out the movement mechanics for the yellow cross. Let me try moving UP.

sections.content:

Plan: Test yellow cross movement and try to collect yellow targets.
